content_en:
“Verily, those who divide their religion and break up into sects (all kinds of religious sects), you (O Muhammad #8) have no concern with them in the least.’"?) So unity of voice and harmony of the hearts are among the most outstanding characteristics of the Saved Sect — Ahlus-Sunnah walJama‘ah, and when differences occur between them arising from Ijtihad in matters in which it is permissible to exercise juristic

1 Ash-Shura 42:13 1 ALAn‘am 6:159

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 42 ey! olsyl gals

reasoning, they bear neither resentment nor enmity, nor hatred towards each other. Rather, they feel that they are brothers, even if this difference occurs between them, so much so that one of them will even pray behind a person whom he considers is not in a state of ritual purity, while the mam considers that he is in a state of ritual purity. For example, when one of them prays behind a person who has eaten camel meat and that Imam considers that it does not invalidate his ablution, while the one who is being led in prayer believes that it does invalidate his ablution, yet he considers his prayer behind that Imam correct, even though, if it were he who was praying it himself, he would consider that his prayer was not correct.

All of this is because they hold the view that differences arising out of Jjtihad in matters that it is permissible to exercise Ijtihad, are not in truth differences. Because each of those who are in disagreement has followed the evidence which he is obliged to follow, evidences it is not permissible for him to renounce. So, they hold the view that their brother, if he disagrees with them, in any particular action, following some evidence, then he is in fact in agreement with them, because they call to compliance with the evidence, wherever it may be. If he disagrees with them, in accordance with some evidence which he has, then he is in truth in agreement with them, because he is acting in conformity with what they call and guide to, which is to judge by the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah #8.

The differences which occurred between the Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, in such matters as these are well known to many of the scholars, even in the time of the Prophet #2 and he did not rebuke any of them. Because when he #2 returned from the Battle of A/-Ahzab and Jibril came to him and directed him to attack Banu Quraizah, who had violated their treaty, he commissioned his Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, saying:

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 43 Baal! sshd

“None of you should pray the ‘Asr prayer except at Banu Quraizah.""! So, they left Al-Madinah for Banu Quraizah and the time for the ‘Asr prayer overtook them. Some of them delayed the ‘Asr prayer until they reached Banu Quraizah after the expiry of the time for it, because the Prophet 2% had said:

“None of you should pray the ‘Asr prayer except at Banu

Quraizah.”” Some of them performed the prayer at its stated time and they said: The Messenger #& wanted us to set out early for Banu Quraizah and he did not want us to delay the prayer past its stated time. They (the latter group) were right, but in spite of this, the Prophet #% did not rebuke anyone among the two groups, and neither of the two groups held any enmity or rancor towards the other, due to their differing in understanding these words.

For this reason, I consider it an obligation upon the Muslims who claim adherence to the Sunnah to be one people, and not to divide into factions, this one belonging to this group and that one belonging to that group and the third belonging to a third group and so on, causing them to attack each other with hostile words, harboring enmity and hatred towards each other due to differences in matters where it is permissible to exercise juristic reasoning (Ijtihad). There is no need to single out every faction by name, but the wise person understands and the matter is

* (1 Reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of Fear, in the Chapter on the Prayer of the One Searching and the One Searched for (946) and it was narrated by Muslim in the Book of Jihad and Maneuvers, in the Chapter on Not Delaying Going Out to Battle (1770).

I consider that it is an obligation upon Ahlus-Sunnah walJama‘ah to unite, even if they disagree in some matters which is necessitated by the evidences according to their understanding. Because there is room for accommodation in these matters, all praise and thanks is to Allah.

The important thing is that hearts should be in harmony and that people should speak with one voice, for there is no doubt that the enemies of the Muslims love to see the Muslims divided, whether they be enemies who openly display their enmity, or enemies who give the appearance of being friends to the Muslims or to Islam while they are not so. So, it is incumbent upon us to be distinguished by this characteristic of the Saved Sect, and that is to speak with one voice.

Q. 7. What is meant by the middle course in religious matters?

A. The middle course in religious matters means that a person should not be excessive, exceeding the limits set by Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, nor be deficient, by not fulfilling what Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High has ordained.

The middle course in religious matters is to hold fast to the way of the Prophet #%, while excess in religious matters is to exceed it, and deficiency is not to attain it.

An example of this is that a man says: I want to stand for the night prayer and I will not sleep any of the time, because prayer is one of the best forms of worship, so I love to spend all the night in prayer. We say: This is excess in the religion of Allah, and it is not right, for something like this happened during the life of the Prophet #%: A number of people met and one of them said: I stand in prayer and I do not sleep, while another said: I fast and I do not break my fast, while a third said: I do not marry women. The Prophet #% was informed of this and he #¥ said:

Fatawa on ‘aqeedah (Creed) 45 Bdedall gg

aly bbls go) oF vss vis Slt cil SU Go

“What is wrong with people who say such and such? I fast and I break my fast, I stand in prayer and I sleep and I marry women,

so whoever dislikes my Sunnah is not from me."

So, these people intended to act excessively in the religion, and the Messenger #% declared that he was innocent of them, because they expressed excessiveness in his Summah which includes fasting, breaking the fast, standing in prayer, sleeping, and marrying women.

As for the one who is deficient, he is the one who says: There is no need for me to perform supererogatory acts of worship, so I shall not do so, but will perform the obligatory acts only. He might also be deficient in the obligatory acts. This is the one who is deficient.

The moderate person is the one who acts in accordance with what the Messenger # and the rightly guided caliphs followed.

Another example would be that of three men before whom is a sinful man, and one of them says: I will not give salutations of peace to this sinner and I will cut him off and stay away from him and not speak to him. The second says: 1 will keep company with this sinner and give salutations of peace to him and smile at him and invite him to my house and accept his invitation, and in my opinion he is not but a righteous man. The third one says: I hate this sinner for his sins and I love him for his faith and I will not cut him off except when cutting him off is a means of improving him, but if cutting him off does not

"1 Reported by Al-Bukhari, in the Book of Good Manners, in the Chapter: Whoever Does not Face the People With Rebuke (6101) and by Muslim in the Book of Marriage, in the Chapter on the Recommendation of Marriage for One Who for One Who Can do so (1401)

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 46 PEM ol yt sald

result in improving him, instead causing him to increase his sinfulness, then I will not cut him off.

We say that the first person is excessive and extreme, while the second is excessively lacking and the third is moderate. This is what we say in all of the acts of worship and in conduct, the people are either deficient or extreme or moderate.

A third example is of a man who is a slave to his wife, she speaks to her husband in any way she wishes and he does not prevent her from doing evil, nor does he encourage her to strive for virtue. She possesses his mind and she becomes master over him. Another man is oppressive and arrogant, and deems himself superior to his wife and he pays no heed to her, and she is even less than a servant in his estimation. A third man is moderate and he treats her as Allah and His Messenger #¢ have commanded him:

‘And they (women) have rights (over their husbands as regards

living expenses) similar (to those of their husbands) over them (as

regards obedience and respect) to what is reasonable,"

‘A believing man should not hate a believing woman. If there is some characteristic in her that he dislikes, there remains some other characteristic in her which pleases him.’'*!

This last one is moderate while the first is extreme in his treatment of his wife and the other is deficient. And you should base all deeds and acts of worship upon this example.

Ol 4LBagarah 2:228 '2] Reported by Muslim in the Book of Suckling, in the Chapter on Advice for Women (1469).

Q. 8. What is the definition of faith according to AhlusSunnah wal-Jama‘ah and does it increase and decrease?

A. Faith, according to Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama‘ah, is to affirm with the heart, with the utterance of the tongue, and with the action of the limbs, so it consists of three elements:

1. Affirmation of the heart. 2. Spoken words. 3. The action of the limbs.

Since this is the case, then it will increase and decrease. This is because affirmation of the heart goes up and down, and believing in something which one is told is not like believing in something which one sees with one’s own eyes, nor is believing the information of one man like believing the information of two men and so on. This is why Ibrahim, peace be upon him, said:
content_id:
"Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang memecah belah agama mereka dan mereka menjadi beberapa golongan, tidak ada sedikitpun tanggung jawabmu (wahai Muhammad ﷺ) terhadap mereka.'" Maka persatuan suara dan keselarasan hati adalah termasuk ciri-ciri paling menonjol dari Firqah Najiyah — Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah, dan ketika perbedaan terjadi di antara mereka yang timbul dari ijtihad dalam masalah-masalah yang diperbolehkan untuk melakukan penalaran fiqh

1 Ash-Shura 42:13 1 Al-An'am 6:159

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 42 ey! olsyl gals

mereka tidak menyimpan kebencian, permusuhan, maupun rasa benci satu sama lain. Sebaliknya, mereka merasa bahwa mereka adalah saudara, meskipun perbedaan ini terjadi di antara mereka, sedemikian rupa sehingga salah seorang dari mereka bahkan akan shalat di belakang orang yang ia anggap tidak dalam keadaan suci, sementara imam tersebut menganggap dirinya dalam keadaan suci. Misalnya, ketika salah seorang dari mereka shalat di belakang seseorang yang telah memakan daging unta dan imam tersebut menganggap hal itu tidak membatalkan wudhunya, sementara orang yang mengikutinya dalam shalat meyakini bahwa hal itu membatalkan wudhunya, namun ia tetap menganggap shalatnya di belakang imam tersebut sah, meskipun jika ia yang mengerjakan shalat sendiri, ia akan menganggap shalatnya tidak sah.

Semua ini karena mereka berpandangan bahwa perbedaan yang timbul dari ijtihad dalam masalah-masalah yang diperbolehkan untuk berijtihad, pada hakikatnya bukanlah perbedaan. Karena setiap orang yang berselisih telah mengikuti dalil yang wajib ia ikuti, dalil yang tidak boleh ia tinggalkan. Maka mereka berpandangan bahwa saudaranya, jika ia berbeda dengan mereka dalam suatu tindakan tertentu dengan mengikuti suatu dalil, maka ia sebenarnya sependapat dengan mereka, karena mereka menyeru kepada ketaatan pada dalil di mana pun adanya. Jika ia berbeda dengan mereka sesuai dengan suatu dalil yang ia miliki, maka ia sejatinya sependapat dengan mereka, karena ia bertindak sesuai dengan apa yang mereka serukan dan tunjukkan, yaitu berhukum dengan Kitab Allah dan Sunnah Rasulullah ﷺ.

Perbedaan yang terjadi di antara para Sahabat, semoga Allah meridhai mereka, dalam masalah-masalah seperti ini sudah diketahui oleh banyak ulama, bahkan pada zaman Nabi ﷺ dan beliau tidak mencela seorang pun dari mereka. Karena ketika beliau ﷺ kembali dari Perang Al-Ahzab dan Jibril datang kepadanya mengarahkannya untuk menyerang Banu Quraizah yang telah melanggar perjanjian mereka, beliau mengutus para Sahabatnya, semoga Allah meridhai mereka, dengan bersabda:

Fatawa on 'Aqidah (Creed) 43 Baal! sshd

"Janganlah seorang pun dari kalian shalat Ashar kecuali di Banu Quraizah."! Maka mereka berangkat dari Al-Madinah menuju Banu Quraizah dan waktu shalat Ashar pun menjumpai mereka. Sebagian dari mereka menunda shalat Ashar hingga mereka tiba di Banu Quraizah setelah waktunya habis, karena Nabi ﷺ telah bersabda:

"Janganlah seorang pun dari kalian shalat Ashar kecuali di Banu

Quraizah."" Sebagian dari mereka mengerjakan shalat pada waktunya yang telah ditentukan dan mereka berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ menghendaki kami untuk segera berangkat ke Banu Quraizah dan beliau tidak menghendaki kami menunda shalat melewati waktunya yang telah ditentukan. Mereka (kelompok yang terakhir) benar, namun meski demikian, Nabi ﷺ tidak mencela seorang pun dari kedua kelompok tersebut, dan tidak satu pun dari kedua kelompok menyimpan permusuhan atau dendam terhadap yang lain, akibat perbedaan mereka dalam memahami sabda ini.

Oleh karena itu, saya memandang sebagai suatu kewajiban bagi kaum Muslimin yang mengaku berpegang teguh pada Sunnah untuk menjadi satu umat, dan tidak terpecah menjadi kelompok-kelompok, yang satu milik kelompok ini dan yang lain milik kelompok itu dan yang ketiga milik kelompok ketiga dan seterusnya, sehingga mereka saling menyerang dengan kata-kata permusuhan, menyimpan kebencian dan permusuhan satu sama lain akibat perbedaan dalam masalah-masalah yang diperbolehkan untuk berijtihad. Tidak perlu menyebut nama setiap kelompok, tetapi orang yang bijak memahami dan masalahnya pun

* (1 Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Rasa Takut, dalam Bab tentang Shalat Orang yang Mencari dan Orang yang Dicari (946) dan diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Jihad dan Taktik Perang, dalam Bab tentang Tidak Menunda Keberangkatan ke Medan Perang (1770).

Saya memandang bahwa merupakan suatu kewajiban bagi Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah untuk bersatu, meskipun mereka berbeda dalam beberapa masalah yang dituntut oleh dalil-dalil menurut pemahaman mereka. Karena ada ruang untuk akomodasi dalam masalah-masalah ini, segala puji dan syukur hanya bagi Allah.

Yang penting adalah hati harus selaras dan manusia harus berbicara dengan satu suara, karena tidak diragukan lagi bahwa musuh-musuh kaum Muslimin sangat menyukai perpecahan di kalangan kaum Muslimin, baik mereka adalah musuh yang terang-terangan menampakkan permusuhan mereka, maupun musuh yang menampakkan diri seolah-olah mereka adalah sahabat bagi kaum Muslimin atau bagi Islam padahal mereka tidak demikian. Maka wajib bagi kita untuk menonjolkan ciri khas Firqah Najiyah ini, yaitu berbicara dengan satu suara.

T. 7. Apa yang dimaksud dengan sikap pertengahan dalam masalah agama?

J. Sikap pertengahan dalam masalah agama berarti seseorang tidak bersikap berlebihan dengan melampaui batas-batas yang telah ditetapkan oleh Allah Yang Maha Agung lagi Maha Kuasa, dan tidak pula bersikap kurang dengan tidak memenuhi apa yang telah diwajibkan oleh Allah Yang Maha Mulia lagi Maha Tinggi.

Sikap pertengahan dalam masalah agama adalah berpegang teguh pada jalan Nabi ﷺ, sementara berlebihan dalam masalah agama adalah melampauinya, dan kekurangan adalah tidak mencapainya.

Contoh hal ini adalah seorang laki-laki yang berkata: Saya ingin melaksanakan shalat malam dan saya tidak akan tidur sedikit pun, karena shalat adalah salah satu bentuk ibadah yang paling utama, maka saya ingin menghabiskan seluruh malam untuk shalat. Kita katakan: Ini adalah sikap berlebihan dalam agama Allah, dan hal ini tidak benar, karena sesuatu seperti ini terjadi semasa hidup Nabi ﷺ: Sejumlah orang berkumpul dan salah seorang dari mereka berkata: Saya shalat malam dan saya tidak tidur, sementara yang lain berkata: Saya berpuasa dan saya tidak berbuka, sementara yang ketiga berkata: Saya tidak menikahi wanita. Nabi ﷺ diberitahu tentang hal ini dan beliau ﷺ bersabda:

Fatawa on 'aqeedah (Creed) 45 Bdedall gg

aly bbls go) oF vss vis Slt cil SU Go

"Ada apa dengan orang-orang yang berkata demikian dan demikian? Saya berpuasa dan saya berbuka, saya shalat malam dan saya tidur, dan saya menikahi wanita, maka barangsiapa yang membenci Sunnahku bukanlah bagian dariku."

Jadi, orang-orang ini bermaksud untuk bersikap berlebihan dalam agama, dan Rasulullah ﷺ menyatakan berlepas diri dari mereka, karena mereka mengungkapkan sikap berlebihan dalam Sunnahnya yang mencakup puasa, berbuka puasa, shalat malam, tidur, dan menikahi wanita.

Adapun orang yang kurang, ia adalah orang yang berkata: Tidak ada kebutuhan bagiku untuk melakukan ibadah-ibadah sunnah, maka saya tidak akan melakukannya, tetapi hanya akan melakukan ibadah-ibadah yang wajib saja. Ia bahkan mungkin juga kurang dalam melaksanakan ibadah-ibadah yang wajib. Inilah orang yang kurang.

Orang yang pertengahan adalah orang yang bertindak sesuai dengan apa yang diikuti oleh Rasulullah ﷺ dan para khalifah yang mendapat petunjuk.

Contoh lain adalah tiga orang laki-laki yang di hadapan mereka terdapat seorang laki-laki yang berbuat dosa, dan salah seorang dari mereka berkata: Saya tidak akan mengucapkan salam kepada pendosa ini, saya akan memutus hubungan dengannya, menjauhinya, dan tidak berbicara kepadanya. Yang kedua berkata: Saya akan berteman dengan pendosa ini, mengucapkan salam kepadanya, tersenyum kepadanya, mengundangnya ke rumah saya, menerima undangannya, dan menurut saya ia tidak lain adalah seorang laki-laki yang saleh. Yang ketiga berkata: Saya membenci pendosa ini karena dosa-dosanya dan saya mencintainya karena imannya, saya tidak akan memutus hubungan dengannya kecuali jika pemutusan hubungan itu merupakan sarana untuk memperbaikinya, tetapi jika pemutusan hubungan tidak

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari, dalam Kitab Adab, dalam Bab: Barangsiapa yang Tidak Menghadapi Manusia dengan Celaan (6101) dan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Nikah, dalam Bab tentang Dianjurkannya Menikah bagi Orang yang Mampu Melakukannya (1401)

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 46 PEM ol yt sald

menghasilkan perbaikan baginya, bahkan menyebabkan ia semakin bertambah dalam kedosaannya, maka saya tidak akan memutus hubungan dengannya.

Kita katakan bahwa orang pertama bersikap berlebihan dan ekstrem, sementara orang kedua terlalu kurang secara berlebihan, dan orang ketiga bersikap pertengahan. Inilah yang kita katakan dalam semua ibadah dan dalam perilaku, manusia terbagi menjadi orang yang kurang, orang yang berlebihan, atau orang yang pertengahan.

Contoh ketiga adalah seorang laki-laki yang menjadi budak bagi istrinya, ia berbicara kepada suaminya sesuka hatinya dan suami tidak mencegahnya dari perbuatan buruk, tidak pula mendorongnya untuk berusaha meraih kebaikan. Istri menguasai pikirannya dan menjadi tuan atasnya. Laki-laki lain bersifat zalim dan sombong, menganggap dirinya lebih tinggi dari istrinya dan tidak memedulikannya sama sekali, bahkan ia menganggap istrinya lebih rendah dari seorang pelayan. Laki-laki ketiga bersikap moderat dan memperlakukannya sebagaimana Allah dan Rasul-Nya ﷺ telah memerintahkannya:

'Dan para wanita mempunyai hak yang seimbang dengan kewajibannya

menurut cara yang ma'ruf,'

'Seorang laki-laki mukmin tidak boleh membenci seorang wanita mukminah. Jika ada satu sifat padanya yang tidak ia sukai, niscaya masih ada sifat lain padanya yang menyenangkannya.'[2]

Yang terakhir ini bersikap moderat, sedangkan yang pertama berlebih-lebihan dalam memperlakukan istrinya dan yang lainnya bersikap lalai. Hendaklah engkau mendasarkan semua amal dan ibadah pada contoh ini.

[1] Al-Baqarah 2:228 [2] Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Penyusuan, pada Bab Nasihat untuk Para Wanita (1469).

T. 8. Apa definisi iman menurut Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah dan apakah iman itu bertambah dan berkurang?

J. Iman, menurut Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah, adalah membenarkan dengan hati, mengucapkan dengan lisan, dan mengamalkan dengan anggota badan, sehingga iman terdiri dari tiga unsur:

1. Pembenaran hati. 2. Ucapan lisan. 3. Amal anggota badan.

Karena demikian halnya, maka iman itu bertambah dan berkurang. Hal ini dikarenakan pembenaran hati naik dan turun, dan beriman kepada sesuatu yang diberitahukan tidaklah sama dengan beriman kepada sesuatu yang disaksikan dengan mata kepala sendiri, begitu pula beriman berdasarkan berita satu orang tidaklah sama dengan beriman berdasarkan berita dua orang dan seterusnya. Inilah sebabnya Ibrahim, 'alaihis-salam, berkata:
content_ar: “Verily, those who divide their religion and break up into sects (all kinds of religious sects), you (O Muhammad #8) have no concern with them in the least.’"?) So unity of voice and harmony of the hearts are among the most outstanding characteristics of the Saved Sect — Ahlus-السنة walJama‘ah, and when differences occur between them arising from الاجتهاد in matters in which it is مباح to exercise juristic 1 Ash-Shura 42:13 1 ALAn‘am 6:159 الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 42 ey! olsyl gals reasoning, they bear neither resentment nor enmity, nor hatred towards each other. Rather, they feel that they are brothers, even if this difference occurs between them, so much so that one of them will even pray behind a person whom he considers is not in a state of ritual purity, while the mam considers that he is in a state of ritual purity. For example, when one of them prays behind a person who has eaten camel meat and that الإمام considers that it does not invalidate his ablution, while the one who is being led in الصلاة believes that it does invalidate his ablution, yet he considers his الصلاة behind that الإمام correct, even though, if it were he who was praying it himself, he would consider that his الصلاة was not correct. All of this is because they hold the view that differences arising out of Jjtihad in matters that it is مباح to exercise الاجتهاد, are not in truth differences. Because each of those who are in disagreement has followed the evidence which he is obliged to follow, evidences it is not مباح for him to renounce. So, they hold the view that their brother, if he disagrees with them, in any particular action, following some evidence, then he is in fact in agreement with them, because they call to compliance with the evidence, wherever it may be. If he disagrees with them, in accordance with some evidence which he has, then he is in truth in agreement with them, because he is acting in conformity with what they call and guide to, which is to judge by the Book of الله and السنة النبوية of رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم #8. The differences which occurred between الصحابة الكرام رضي الله عنهم, رضي الله عنهم, in such matters as these are well known to many of the العلماء, even in the time of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #2 and he did not rebuke any of them. Because when he #2 returned from the Battle of A/-Ahzab and Jibril came to him and directed him to attack Banu Quraizah, who had violated their treaty, he commissioned his الصحابة, رضي الله عنهم, saying: الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 43 Baal! sshd “None of you should pray the ‘Asr الصلاة except at Banu Quraizah.""! So, they left Al-المدينة المنورة for Banu Quraizah and the time for the ‘Asr الصلاة overtook them. Some of them delayed the ‘Asr الصلاة until they reached Banu Quraizah after the expiry of the time for it, because النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 2% had said: “None of you should pray the ‘Asr الصلاة except at Banu Quraizah.”” Some of them performed the الصلاة at its stated time and they said: The Messenger #& wanted us to set out early for Banu Quraizah and he did not want us to delay the الصلاة past its stated time. They (the latter group) were right, but in spite of this, النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% did not rebuke anyone among the two groups, and neither of the two groups held any enmity or rancor towards the other, due to their differing in understanding these words. For this reason, I consider it an obligation upon the المسلمون who claim adherence to السنة النبوية to be one people, and not to divide into factions, this one belonging to this group and that one belonging to that group and the third belonging to a third group and so on, causing them to attack each other with hostile words, harboring enmity and hatred towards each other due to differences in matters where it is مباح to exercise juristic reasoning (الاجتهاد). There is no need to single out every faction by name, but the wise person understands and the matter is * (1 Reported by البخاري in the Book of Fear, in the Chapter on the الصلاة of the One Searching and the One Searched for (946) and it was روى by المسلم in the Book of الجهاد and Maneuvers, in the Chapter on Not Delaying Going Out to Battle (1770). I consider that it is an obligation upon Ahlus-السنة walJama‘ah to unite, even if they disagree in some matters which is necessitated by the evidences according to their understanding. Because there is room for accommodation in these matters, all praise and thanks is to الله. The important thing is that hearts should be in harmony and that people should speak with one voice, for there is no doubt that the enemies of the المسلمون love to see the المسلمون divided, whether they be enemies who openly display their enmity, or enemies who give the appearance of being friends to the المسلمون or to الإسلام while they are not so. So, it is incumbent upon us to be distinguished by this characteristic of the Saved Sect, and that is to speak with one voice. Q. 7. What is meant by the middle course in religious matters? A. The middle course in religious matters means that a person should not be excessive, exceeding the limits set by الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful, nor be deficient, by not fulfilling what الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High has ordained. The middle course in religious matters is to hold fast to the way of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #%, while excess in religious matters is to exceed it, and deficiency is not to attain it. An example of this is that a man says: I want to stand for the night الصلاة and I will not sleep any of the time, because الصلاة is one of the best forms of worship, so I love to spend all the night in الصلاة. We say: This is excess in the religion of الله, and it is not right, for something like this happened during the life of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #%: A number of people met and one of them said: I stand in الصلاة and I do not sleep, while another said: I fast and I do not break my fast, while a third said: I do not marry women. النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #% was informed of this and he #¥ said: الفتاوى on ‘aqeedah (Creed) 45 Bdedall gg aly bbls go) oF vss vis Slt cil SU Go “What is wrong with people who say such and such? I fast and I break my fast, I stand in الصلاة and I sleep and I marry women, so whoever dislikes my السنة is not from me." So, these people intended to act excessively in the religion, and the Messenger #% declared that he was innocent of them, because they expressed excessiveness in his Summah which includes الصيام, breaking the fast, standing in الصلاة, sleeping, and marrying women. As for the one who is deficient, he is the one who says: There is no need for me to perform supererogatory acts of worship, so I shall not do so, but will perform the واجب acts only. He might also be deficient in the واجب acts. This is the one who is deficient. The moderate person is the one who acts in accordance with what the Messenger # and the rightly guided caliphs followed. Another example would be that of three men before whom is a sinful man, and one of them says: I will not give salutations of peace to this sinner and I will cut him off and stay away from him and not speak to him. The second says: 1 will keep company with this sinner and give salutations of peace to him and smile at him and invite him to my house and accept his invitation, and in my opinion he is not but a righteous man. The third one says: I hate this sinner for his sins and I love him for his الإيمان and I will not cut him off except when cutting him off is a means of improving him, but if cutting him off does not "1 Reported by البخاري, in the Book of Good Manners, in the Chapter: Whoever Does not Face the People With Rebuke (6101) and by المسلم in the Book of النكاح, in the Chapter on the Recommendation of النكاح for One Who for One Who Can do so (1401) الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 46 PEM ol yt sald result in improving him, instead causing him to increase his sinfulness, then I will not cut him off. We say that the first person is excessive and extreme, while the second is excessively lacking and the third is moderate. This is what we say in all of the acts of worship and in conduct, the people are either deficient or extreme or moderate. A third example is of a man who is a slave to his wife, she speaks to her husband in any way she wishes and he does not prevent her from doing evil, nor does he encourage her to strive for الفضيلة. She possesses his mind and she becomes master over him. Another man is oppressive and arrogant, and deems himself superior to his wife and he pays no heed to her, and she is even less than a servant in his estimation. A third man is moderate and he treats her as الله and His Messenger #¢ have commanded him: ‘And they (women) have rights (over their husbands as regards living expenses) similar (to those of their husbands) over them (as regards obedience and respect) to what is reasonable," ‘A believing man should not hate a believing woman. If there is some characteristic in her that he dislikes, there remains some other characteristic in her which pleases him.’'*! This last one is moderate while the first is extreme in his treatment of his wife and the other is deficient. And you should base all deeds and acts of worship upon this example. Ol 4LBagarah 2:228 '2] Reported by المسلم in the Book of Suckling, in the Chapter on Advice for Women (1469). Q. 8. What is the definition of الإيمان according to AhlusSunnah wal-Jama‘ah and does it increase and decrease? A. الإيمان, according to Ahlus-السنة wal-Jama‘ah, is to affirm with the heart, with the utterance of the tongue, and with the action of the limbs, so it consists of three elements: 1. Affirmation of the heart. 2. Spoken words. 3. The action of the limbs. Since this is the case, then it will increase and decrease. This is because affirmation of the heart goes up and down, and believing in something which one is told is not like believing in something which one sees with one’s own eyes, nor is believing the information of one man like believing the information of two men and so on. This is why Ibrahim, peace be upon him, said:
